content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/components/drawer/record/record-panel.css

Companion test files — you MUST read these after applying fixes:
- /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/components/drawer/record/__tests__/record-panel.spec.tsx
After fixing the main file, read each test file above and check if any test:
- Opens a dropdown/menu/popover and queries for content (getByText, getByRole)
- Uses querySelector with OUIA selectors to interact with components
- Asserts on element roles, aria attributes, or data attributes that changed
If any test would break due to your changes, fix it. Common test fixes:
- Wrap assertions in waitFor() when content renders via portal
- Add popperProps={{ appendTo: 'inline' }} to component renders in tests
- Update getByRole/getByText queries to match new attribute values

Apply ALL of the following 2 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 1
Rule [sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-content]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-content' was removed in the new version. A simple version prefix swap to 'pf-v6-c-content' does NOT produce a valid class — this class does not exist in the target CSS distribution.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a PatternFly component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-content

Code context:
```
    1  .pf-v5-c-content.record-field-container {
    2    margin-bottom: 10px;
    3  }

```

### Fix 2
Line: 1
Rule [semver-css-class-removed-c-content]:
The CSS class 'pf-v5-c-content' has no equivalent in the new version. There is no valid 'pf-v6-c-content' class — this class was removed, not renamed.

Remove this class reference or replace it with appropriate custom CSS or a component prop.

Incident context:
  className: pf-v5-c-content

Code context:
```
    1  .pf-v5-c-content.record-field-container {
    2    margin-bottom: 10px;
    3  }

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/netobserv-web-console/source/web/src/components/drawer/record/record-panel.css
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.
I'll start by reading the CSS file and the companion test file.
Now let me also check how `.record-field-container` is used in the component to understand the context:

Now I understand the full picture. Let me reason through the fixes:

**Fix 1 & Fix 2** (both target the same thing): Line 1 uses `.pf-v5-c-content.record-field-container`. The `pf-v5-c-content` class was removed in PF6 with no equivalent `pf-v6-c-content`. The CSS rule applies `margin-bottom: 10px` to elements that have both classes. Since `pf-v5-c-content` no longer exists in the design system, the selector will never match elements styled by PatternFly. 

Looking at the component code (line 440 of record-panel.tsx), the element only has `className="record-field-container ..."` — it doesn't explicitly add `pf-v5-c-content`. The `pf-v5-c-content` class was likely inherited from a parent PatternFly `Content` component. Since the class is gone in PF6, I should simplify the selector to just `.record-field-container` so the `margin-bottom` style still applies.

The test file queries `.record-field-container` — that class is unchanged, so tests won't break.
Let me verify the final file:

The file looks correct. Now let me verify the test file doesn't need changes:

The test file (`record-panel.spec.tsx`) queries `.record-field-container` via `document.querySelectorAll('.record-field-container')`. Since I only removed the `pf-v5-c-content` prefix from the CSS selector (not the class itself), and the component code at line 440 of `record-panel.tsx` applies `className="record-field-container ..."` directly, the tests will continue to find elements with that class. No test changes needed.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Detail |
|-----|------|--------|--------|
| Fix 1 | `sd-css-dead-class-pf-v5-c-content` | ✅ Applied | Removed `pf-v5-c-content` from the compound selector `.pf-v5-c-content.record-field-container` on line 1, simplifying it to `.record-field-container`. The `pf-v5-c-content` class was removed in PF6 with no equivalent, so the compound selector would never match. The `margin-bottom: 10px` style is preserved and now applies directly to `.record-field-container` elements. |
| Fix 2 | `semver-css-class-removed-c-content` | ✅ Applied | Same change as Fix 1 — both fixes target the identical `pf-v5-c-content` reference on line 1. Resolved by the single edit above. |

**Test file impact**: No changes needed to `record-panel.spec.tsx`. Tests query `.record-field-container` which is unchanged. The removed `pf-v5-c-content` class was not referenced in any test assertions.
